blocks.text: Perfect! Now let me run the DESeq2 analysis in R:

input.code:
library(DESeq2)
library(ggplot2)

# Read counts and metadata
counts_df <- read.csv('counts_de.csv', row.names = 1)
metadata_df <- read.csv('metadata_de.csv', row.names = 1)

# Ensure counts are integers and cells match
counts_df <- as.matrix(counts_df)
storage.mode(counts_df) <- "integer"

print(paste("Counts matrix:", nrow(counts_df), "genes ×", ncol(counts_df), "cells"))
print(paste("Metadata rows:", nrow(metadata_df)))
print(table(metadata_df$cell_type))

# Create DESeqDataSet
dds <- DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(countData = counts_df,
                               colData = metadata_df,
                               design = ~ cell_type)

print("DESeqDataSet created")
print(dds)
